title: ObjectiveContract
description: Goal-completion governance contract. Required when Plan.goal_type ∈ {parity, new_feature, migration, incident}. Describes the user-facing outcome the plan must satisfy, separate from the mechanical artifacts produced (which features.json describes). The plan-level validator enforces that goal_type here matches Plan.goal_type when both are present.

goal_type.description: Must match Plan.goal_type at the parent plan when both are present. The four values that require an objective contract per Goal Governance V1 §4.

source_of_truth.description: Reference to the canonical source (PRD path, source-platform name for parity, incident report URL, prior plan id, etc.). Does NOT need to be a live repo URL; static curated references are valid.

user_journeys.description: User-facing flows the goal must satisfy end-to-end. The auditor walks these against the proposed features at pre-impl time and against shipped patches at post-impl time.

description.description: What the user does, end-to-end. Substantive enough that the auditor can reason about coverage gaps.

states.description: States the journey transitions through (e.g., 'empty', 'loading', 'error', 'success'). Optional.

acceptance_signals.description: What the auditor should look for to confirm journey coverage. Optional but recommended.

required_evidence.description: Evidence the post-impl audit (Plan F2) must produce (screenshots, logs, journey walks). Optional at pre-impl time; load-bearing at post-impl.

non_goals.description: Explicit out-of-scope items. Prevents scope-creep findings during sufficiency audit and integration audit.

completion_test.description: One-sentence prose answering 'how do we know this is done?'. The completion test is the operator's stake in the ground; the auditor checks the proposed features against it.

cluster_overrides.description: Optional override notes for the Goal Governance V1 §6.3 cluster coherence rule. Free-form for v1; future versions may structure this. Only set when the default rule (subsystem_and_journey intersection) doesn't fit the plan's gap-clustering pattern.
